steps.title: Fastest path: mark the figure first
steps.content: Label the side pair for the named angle first: opposite, adjacent, and hypotenuse. Then pick the matching shortcut, such as SOH/CAH/TOA, complementary trig values, or a special right-triangle ratio.

steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: Since ABC ~ DEF with A corresponding to D, the angles are equal, so sin D = sin A. The numbers 35, 612, 613 form a Pythagorean triple: 35² + 612² = 613². In right triangle ABC with ∠C = 90°, this means the two legs are 35 and 612, and the hypotenuse is 613. If the side opposite A = 612 and the hypotenuse = 613, then sin A = opposite/hypotenuse = 612/613. The given ratio 612/613 is therefore interpreted as sin A (not tan A), consistent with the 35-612-613 right triangle. Because sin D = sin A, we have sin D = 612/613.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — $\frac{612}{613}$: sin D = sin A = opposite/hypotenuse = 612/613. ✓
  • Choice B — $\frac{35}{613}$: This equals cos A (adjacent/hypotenuse), not sin A. ✗
  • Choice C — $\frac{613}{612}$: Greater than 1 — impossible for any sine value. ✗
  • Choice D — $\frac{613}{35}$: Greater than 1 — impossible for any sine value. ✗
That confirms A is correct.
